define i64 @pr82271(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %b
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %sa = sext i32 %a to i64
  %sb = sext i32 %b to i64
  %add = add nsw i64 %sa, 1
  %smax = call i64 @llvm.smax.i64(i64 %sb, i64 %add)
  ret i64 %smax

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i64 0
}

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %sa = zext nneg i32 %a to i64
  %sb = zext nneg i32 %b to i64
  %add = add nsw i64 %sa, 1

declare i64 @llvm.smax.i64(i64, i64) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

define i1 @cmp_sext(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %b
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %sa = sext i32 %a to i64
  %sb = sext i32 %b to i64
  %add = add nsw i64 %sa, 1
  %cmp2 = icmp sge i64 %sb, %add
  ret i1 %cmp2

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

define i1 @cmp_sext_sgt(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %b
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %sa = sext i32 %a to i64
  %sb = sext i32 %b to i64
  %add = add nsw i64 %sa, 1
  %cmp2 = icmp sgt i64 %sb, %add
  ret i1 %cmp2

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

define i1 @cmp_sext_dynamic_increment(i32 %a, i32 %b, i64 %c) {
entry:
  %pos = icmp slt i64 %c, 2
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %pos)
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %b
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %sa = sext i32 %a to i64
  %sb = sext i32 %b to i64
  %add = add nsw i64 %sa, %c
  %cmp2 = icmp sge i64 %sb, %add
  ret i1 %cmp2

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(inaccessiblemem: write) }

define i1 @cmp_sext_add(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %b
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %a1 = add nsw i32 %a, 1
  %b1 = add nsw i32 %b, 1
  %sa = sext i32 %a1 to i64
  %sb = sext i32 %b1 to i64
  %add = add nsw i64 %sa, 1
  %cmp2 = icmp sge i64 %sb, %add
  ret i1 %cmp2

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

define i1 @cmp_zext_nneg(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:

  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:                                             ; preds = %entry

  %sb = zext nneg i32 %b to i64
  %add = add nsw i64 %sa, 1
  %cmp2 = icmp sge i64 %sb, %add
  ret i1 %cmp2

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  ret i1 false
}

issue.title: [ConstraintElimination] Missed optimization: fold `max(SExt(b), SExt(a) + 1)` to `SExt(b)` if `a s< b`
issue.body:
Alive2 proof: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/A8dtGp

### Motivating example 
Similar to #75155
```llvm
define i64 @src(i32 %a, i32 %b){
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %b
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:
  %sa = sext i32 %a to i64
  %sb = sext i32 %b to i64
  %add = add i64 %sa, 1
  %smax = call i64 @llvm.smax.i64(i64 %sb, i64 %add)
  call void @dummy()
  ret i64 %smax

else:
  ret i64 0
}
```

can be folded to:
```llvm
define i64 @tgt(i32 %a, i32 %b){
entry:
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %b
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %else

then:
  %sb = sext i32 %b to i64
  call void @dummy()
  ret i64 %sb

else:
  ret i64 0
}
```

### Real-world motivation

This snippet of IR is derived from [redis/src/db.c@xreadGetKeys](https://github.com/redis/redis/blob/6df42df2917622cfe791b0f563fa82053965820f/src/db.c#L2554) (after O3 pipeline).
The example above is a reduced version. If you're interested in the original suboptimal IR and optimal IR, see also:https://godbolt.org/z/Kz4M6WPxn

**Let me know if you can confirm that it's an optimization opportunity, thanks.**
issue.author: XChy

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: Initial support for simplifying min/max has been added in https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/75306, not sure exactly what is missing. cc @zjaffal

comments.author: XChy
comments.body:
It seemed that zext-like insts had been decomposed before, but opt on godbolt didn't optimize it away when I opened this issue...
Correct this issue now.

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: Thanks for clarifying re unsigned, I was surprised that this wasn’t handled yet
